description: Fix single-recipe grocery list bug (ingredients don't display when only 1 recipe), clean up dead code (groceryEdits.ts, display_name_map column), auto-add new Google sign-in users as viewers, and maintain 100% test coverage.

userStories.title: Fix single-recipe grocery list not displaying
userStories.description: As a user, I want the grocery list to display ingredients even when there's only 1 parsed recipe, so that I can see a shopping list for a single recipe in both Events and Meal Plans.
acceptanceCriteria: EventDetailPage.tsx: runSmartCombine runs for 1+ parsed recipes (change `parsedRecipes.length < 2` to `< 1`)
MealPlanPage.tsx: runSmartCombine runs for 1+ parsed recipes (same fix)
EventDetailPage.tsx parse flow: willCombine logic calls runSmartCombine even for the first recipe (change `existingParsedCount >= 1` to `>= 0`)
MealPlanPage.tsx parse flow: shouldCombine logic calls runSmartCombine even for 1 recipe (change `recipesWithUrls.length >= 2` to `>= 1`)
showCombineStep in parse progress still only shows 'Combining with other recipes' step when 2+ recipes (this is a UX label, not the logic gate)
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
npm run test:coverage passes with 100% on required directories
userStories.notes: Two-layer bug. Layer 1: `runSmartCombine` in both pages has `parsedRecipes.length < 2` at EventDetailPage.tsx:421 and MealPlanPage.tsx:272 — change to `< 1`. Layer 2: The parse flow guards also skip calling runSmartCombine for single recipes. EventDetailPage.tsx:598 has `willCombine = existingParsedCount >= 1` — change to `>= 0` so the first recipe gets combined. MealPlanPage.tsx:350 has `shouldCombine = recipesWithUrls.length >= 2` — change to `>= 1`. IMPORTANT: Keep the `showCombineStep` UI label gated on 2+ recipes — showing 'Combining with other recipes' for a single recipe would be confusing. So split the logic: always call runSmartCombine, but only show the progress step label when there are multiple recipes.

userStories.title: Update tests for single-recipe grocery behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify single-recipe grocery display works correctly, so that the fix from US-001 is covered.
acceptanceCriteria: EventDetailPage.test.tsx: new test verifying smart combine IS called when 1 parsed recipe exists
EventDetailPage.test.tsx: existing test for 2+ recipes still passes
MealPlanPage.test.tsx: new test verifying smart combine IS called when 1 parsed recipe exists
MealPlanPage.test.tsx: update or remove test 'skips smart combine when fewer than 2 parsed recipes' — this behavior no longer applies
MealPlanPage.test.tsx: test that parse flow calls runSmartCombine for a single URL recipe
npm run test:coverage passes with 100% on required directories
npm run test:run passes (all tests green)
userStories.notes: MealPlanPage.test.tsx line ~2575 has a test 'skips smart combine when fewer than 2 parsed recipes' that explicitly verifies the old (buggy) behavior — this test must be updated. EventDetailPage.test.tsx line ~5001 has 'runs smart combine on cache miss with 2+ parsed recipes' — add a companion test for 1 parsed recipe. For MealPlanPage, also check line ~3525 'runs combine step during parse when 2+ recipes with URLs on groceries tab' and add a test for 1 recipe. The showCombineStep progress label should still only appear for 2+ recipes — add assertions for that.

userStories.title: Delete dead groceryEdits module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to commit the deletion of groceryEdits.ts and its tests since the module is dead code (never imported in production).
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/groceryEdits.ts is deleted (already staged via git rm)
tests/unit/lib/groceryEdits.test.ts is deleted (already staged via git rm)
No references to groceryEdits remain in src/
npm run build passes
npm run test:coverage passes with 100% on required directories
userStories.notes: Both files are already staged for deletion via git rm. Just need to verify no remaining references and include in the commit. groceryEdits.ts (149 lines) contained loadCombinedGroceryItems, saveCombinedGroceryItems, updateGroceryItem, addCustomGroceryItem, removeGroceryItem — all duplicating groceryCache.ts functionality and never imported in production code.

userStories.title: Drop unused display_name_map column
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to drop the unused display_name_map column from combined_grocery_items since it was superseded by per_recipe_items.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file created: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_drop_display_name_map.sql
Migration drops display_name_map column from combined_grocery_items table
No references to display_name_map exist in any .ts/.tsx files
Migration applied to production Supabase project (bluilkrggkspxsnehfez)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The display_name_map column was added by migration 20260225000001_add_display_name_map_to_grocery_cache.sql. It was replaced by per_recipe_items (migration 20260226100000). Grep confirms zero references to display_name_map or displayNameMap in any source files. Use: ALTER TABLE combined_grocery_items DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS display_name_map; Apply to production via Supabase MCP tool (project: bluilkrggkspxsnehfez).

userStories.title: Auto-add new users as viewers on sign-up
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to automatically get viewer access when I sign in with Google, so that I don't see an 'Access Denied' screen and can immediately use the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_auto_add_allowed_users.sql
handle_new_user() function updated to also INSERT INTO allowed_users with email=NEW.email, role='viewer', is_club_member=false, invited_by=NULL
Uses ON CONFLICT (email) DO NOTHING so pre-invited users keep their existing role/membership
Migration applied to production Supabase project (bluilkrggkspxsnehfez)
New Google sign-in user auto-appears as viewer, not club member
Pre-invited users keep their existing role and club membership unchanged
Admin can still promote viewers to admin via /users page
userStories.notes: Existing handle_new_user() is at baseline migration 20260117000000_baseline_schema.sql lines 114-132. It currently only inserts into profiles. Add an INSERT INTO public.allowed_users (email, role, is_club_member) VALUES (NEW.email, 'viewer', false) ON CONFLICT (email) DO NOTHING; after the profiles insert. The allowed_users table (baseline:20-27) has: id UUID PK, email TEXT UNIQUE, role TEXT ('admin'|'viewer'), is_club_member BOOLEAN, invited_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. The ON CONFLICT ensures pre-invited users (who already have rows with possibly admin role or club membership) are not overwritten. Apply via Supabase MCP tool.

userStories.title: Verify and fix 100% test coverage on all required directories
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to ensure 100% test coverage on all required directories after all prior stories are complete, fixing any gaps introduced by the changes.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:coverage shows 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines for src/components/events/
npm run test:coverage shows 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines for src/components/ingredients/
npm run test:coverage shows 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines for src/components/mealplan/
npm run test:coverage shows 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines for src/components/recipes/
npm run test:coverage shows 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines for src/lib/
src/components/wheel/IngredientWheel.tsx is exempt (~55% acceptable)
npm run test:run passes (all tests green)
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Run npm run test:coverage and inspect the output. Current state before this PRD: all required directories are at 100% statement coverage, with minor branch gaps in AddMealDialog.tsx (line 118), MealPlanPage.tsx (line 460), and RecipeHub.tsx (line 932). After the changes from US-001/US-002, new branches may be introduced that need coverage. If any file in the required directories is below 100%, add or update tests to close the gap. The pre-commit hook enforces this automatically, so this story is a final safety net. Also run npm run build and npm run lint to confirm everything is clean.
